audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for one lip/language, Babel wordplay, city/tower/name-making, divine descent, scattering, and Shem-to-Abram genealogy. Final source-critical review may refine chronology and name traditions.

verses.restored: And all the earth had one language and one set of words.

familiar.text: And the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech.

notes.title: One language
notes.body: Literally one lip and one set of words. The unity is linguistic before it becomes architectural and political.

verses.restored: As they journeyed from the east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar...

notes.title: Shinar
notes.body: Shinar links this story to Babel and the kingdom beginnings named in Genesis 10.

verses.restored: They said each to his neighbor, Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly. They had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.

familiar.text: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them throughly...

notes.title: Brick and bitumen
notes.body: The verse highlights human technology and construction materials.

verses.restored: And they said, Come, let us build for ourselves a city and a tower, with its head in the heavens, and let us make for ourselves a name, lest we be scattered over the face of all the earth.

familiar.text: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top may reach unto heaven...

notes.title: Make a name
notes.body: The desire is self-made permanence and reputation. This contrasts later with Abram, whose name will be made by divine promise.

notes.title: Lest we be scattered
notes.body: They build to prevent dispersion, resisting the spread over the earth described in Genesis 10.

verses.restored: And the Eternal came down to see the city and the tower that the children of the human had built.

familiar.text: And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.

notes.title: Came down
notes.body: The tower aimed toward heaven, yet the Eternal must come down to see it. The narrative undercuts human grandiosity.

verses.restored: And the Eternal said, Look, one people and one language for all of them, and this is what they begin to do. Now nothing they plan to do will be withheld from them.

familiar.text: And the LORD said, Behold, the people is one, and they have all one language...

notes.title: Unchecked capacity
notes.body: The concern is not cooperation itself, but unified human power oriented toward self-exalting control.

verses.restored: Come, let us go down and confuse their language there, so that each one will not hear the language of his neighbor.

familiar.text: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another's speech.

notes.title: Let us go down
notes.body: The divine plural echoes earlier plural speech. Confusing language interrupts centralized self-exalting power.

verses.restored: So the Eternal scattered them from there over the face of all the earth, and they ceased building the city.

familiar.text: So the LORD scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.

notes.title: Scattered
notes.body: What they feared in verse 4 happens through divine disruption.

verses.restored: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the Eternal confused the language of all the earth, and from there the Eternal scattered them over the face of all the earth.

familiar.text: Therefore is the name of it called Babel; because the LORD did there confound the language of all the earth...

notes.title: Babel wordplay
notes.body: Babel is linked with balal, confuse. The city of name-making becomes the place of confused language.

verses.restored: These are the generations of Shem. Shem was one hundred years old and fathered Arpachshad two years after the flood.

familiar.text: These are the generations of Shem: Shem was an hundred years old, and begat Arphaxad two years after the flood:

notes.title: Shem genealogy
notes.body: The narrative narrows from all nations toward Abram’s line.

verses.restored: After Shem fathered Arpachshad, he lived five hundred years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Shem lived after he begat Arphaxad five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

verses.restored: Arpachshad lived thirty-five years and fathered Shelah.

familiar.text: And Arphaxad lived five and thirty years, and begat Salah:

verses.restored: After Arpachshad fathered Shelah, he lived four hundred three years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Salah four hundred and three years...

verses.restored: Shelah lived thirty years and fathered Eber.

familiar.text: And Salah lived thirty years, and begat Eber:

verses.restored: After Shelah fathered Eber, he lived four hundred three years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Salah lived after he begat Eber four hundred and three years...

verses.restored: Eber lived thirty-four years and fathered Peleg.

familiar.text: And Eber lived four and thirty years, and begat Peleg:

verses.restored: After Eber fathered Peleg, he lived four hundred thirty years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Eber lived after he begat Peleg four hundred and thirty years...

verses.restored: Peleg lived thirty years and fathered Reu.

familiar.text: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:

verses.restored: After Peleg fathered Reu, he lived two hundred nine years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Peleg lived after he begat Reu two hundred and nine years...

verses.restored: Reu lived thirty-two years and fathered Serug.

familiar.text: And Reu lived two and thirty years, and begat Serug:

verses.restored: After Reu fathered Serug, he lived two hundred seven years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Reu lived after he begat Serug two hundred and seven years...

verses.restored: Serug lived thirty years and fathered Nahor.

familiar.text: And Serug lived thirty years, and begat Nahor:

verses.restored: After Serug fathered Nahor, he lived two hundred years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Serug lived after he begat Nahor two hundred years...

verses.restored: Nahor lived twenty-nine years and fathered Terah.

familiar.text: And Nahor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Terah:

verses.restored: After Nahor fathered Terah, he lived one hundred nineteen years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Nahor lived after he begat Terah an hundred and nineteen years...

verses.restored: Terah lived seventy years and fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

familiar.text: And Terah lived seventy years, and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

notes.title: Abram introduced
notes.body: The genealogy reaches Abram, the next major movement in Genesis.

verses.restored: These are the generations of Terah. Terah fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran fathered Lot.

familiar.text: Now these are the generations of Terah: Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran...

verses.restored: Haran died before the face of Terah his father in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.

familiar.text: And Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.

notes.title: Before the face of Terah
notes.body: The phrase means during his father’s lifetime or in his father’s presence.

verses.restored: Abram and Nahor took women for themselves. The name of Abram’s woman was Sarai, and the name of Nahor’s woman was Milcah, daughter of Haran, father of Milcah and father of Iscah.

familiar.text: And Abram and Nahor took them wives: the name of Abram's wife was Sarai...

verses.restored: Now Sarai was barren; she had no child.

familiar.text: But Sarai was barren; she had no child.

notes.title: Barren
notes.body: Sarai’s barrenness sets up the promise story that begins in Genesis 12.

verses.restored: Terah took Abram his son, Lot son of Haran his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, the woman of Abram his son; and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to the land of Canaan. They came as far as Haran and settled there.

familiar.text: And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot... and Sarai... and they went forth... from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan...

notes.title: Toward Canaan
notes.body: The movement toward Canaan begins before Abram’s call is narrated.

verses.restored: The days of Terah were two hundred five years, and Terah died in Haran.

familiar.text: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years: and Terah died in Haran.
